product_overview: EYE-ASSISST is a web-based AI screening platform that analyzes fundus photographs to detect six retinal diseases, provides Grad-CAM explainability for clinician review, and offers role-based portals for patients, technicians, doctors, and admins.
core_goals: Allow technicians to upload fundus images and receive AI analysis (disease probabilities, severity, Grad-CAM heatmap) in a simple workflow
Enable doctors to review AI results with visual explanations, record clinical notes, and approve/modify/override AI assessments
Provide patients access to their scan history, downloadable PDF reports, and the ability to book follow-up appointments
Give admins visibility into platform analytics and basic user account management without backend complexity
Make multi-role workflows (Technician → AI → Doctor → Patient) streamlined and auditable in the UI

features.name: Public Landing Page
features.description: Introduce the product, showcase how the AI workflow works, and direct users to role-based login.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See hero section with interactive 3D eye model and how-it-works explainer -> Review AI disease coverage and research metrics -> Choose to get started -> Navigate to /login

features.name: Authentication (Role-based Login & Signup)
features.description: Let users choose a role (Patient / Doctor / Technician / Admin) and sign in or sign up with validated credentials; session stored client-side to enable role-specific dashboards.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Select role -> Fill email and password -> Submit to login -> See /dashboard for the selected role
Navigate to /login -> Select role -> Fill sign-up fields (name, phone, email, password, confirm password) -> Submit sign-up -> See /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Select role -> Fill email with invalid format -> Submit -> See email validation error

features.name: Patient Portal (Scan History, Reports, Appointments)
features.description: Allow patients to view their scan history, download formatted PDF reports, book follow-ups, and update basic profile information.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Select Patient role -> Fill email and password -> Submit to login -> See /dashboard
See scan history list on dashboard -> Select a scan to view details -> View AI diagnosis summary and doctor's notes -> Download report as PDF -> Observe browser download
See scan history list on dashboard -> Open Book Appointment workflow -> Select specialty and time slot -> Complete booking flow -> See booking confirmation
Navigate to /login -> Select Patient role -> Fill profile fields with mismatched passwords -> Submit profile changes -> See password confirmation validation error

features.name: Technician Upload & Analysis
features.description: Enable technicians to upload fundus images with patient metadata, validate files client-side, and submit images for AI analysis that returns multi-disease probabilities, severity, and Grad-CAM heatmap.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Select Technician role -> Fill email and password -> Submit to login -> See /dashboard

Open technician upload workflow on dashboard -> Provide patient metadata -> Drag or select a fundus image file -> Observe client-side file validation result -> Submit image for analysis -> See AI result with binary outcome, severity label, confidence percentage, multi-disease probabilities, original image, and heatmap
Open technician upload workflow on dashboard -> Provide patient metadata -> Select a file with unsupported MIME type -> Observe validation error and reject file

features.name: Doctor Review Portal
features.description: Let doctors examine pending scans, compare original images with Grad-CAM heatmaps and vessel overlay, record a clinical decision (Approve / Modify / Reject) with notes, and submit a review that updates the pending list.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Select Doctor role -> Fill email and password -> Submit to login -> See /dashboard

See pending scans list on dashboard -> Select a pending scan to open review viewport -> View original image and Grad-CAM heatmap side-by-side -> Toggle vessel overlay -> Choose review action (Approve/Modify/Reject) -> Enter clinical notes -> Submit review -> See scan removed from pending list
See pending scans list on dashboard -> Select a pending scan -> Choose Modify action -> Update severity and notes -> Submit review -> See updated assessment recorded in the dashboard

features.name: Admin Analytics & User Management
features.description: Provide admins with platform analytics dashboards and a local user-management panel to add, search, toggle, and remove users persisted to client storage for demo/admin workflows.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Select Admin role -> Fill email and password -> Submit to login -> See /dashboard
Open Admin analytics on dashboard -> View key metrics (total scans, severity distribution, override rate) -> Inspect daily screening volume and model performance charts
Open User Management on dashboard -> Open Add User form -> Fill name, email, phone, role -> Submit to add user -> See new user appear in the user list
Open User Management on dashboard -> Use role filter to filter users -> Toggle a user's Active/Inactive state -> See state updated in the list

routes.description: User role selection and login/signup page
Main dashboard handling patient, doctor, tech, and admin views

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Login and signup for Patient, Doctor, Technician, and Admin roles.

features.name: Patient Portal
features.description: View scan history, book appointments, and edit profile.
user_interactions: Click on scan to view details

features.name: Doctor Portal
features.description: View pending scans, run Grad-CAM heatmap, write clinical review, and approve scans.

features.name: Admin Portal
features.description: View analytics and manage user accounts.
